exact_quote: vertical drop 3,450 ft (1,052 m) ... Skiable area 5,289 acres (8.3 sq mi; 21.4 km2) ... Lifts 31 ... Longest run Riva Ridge, 4 mi (6.4 km) ... Snowfall 354 in (29.5 ft; 9.0 m)
notes: Massive flagship CO resort: 3,450ft vert, 5,289 acres (3rd largest single-mountain US), 31 lifts, 354in avg snowfall, Back Bowls + Blue Sky Basin glades. EGE Eagle County ~30mi closest commercial, DEN ~100mi east.

exact_quote: just over 500 feet of vertical drop on the hill ... 2 lifts transport the guests. More specifically, it features a triple chair that was purchased from Belleayre over a decade ago and a beginner slope served by a rope tow ... three lines that are maintained have 100% snowmaking capacity
notes: Army-operated ski area at West Point NY (est. 1939, Army's oldest). 500ft vert, 2 lifts (triple chair + rope tow), 100% snowmaking. Located on West Point Golf Course. SWF Stewart ~30mi south.

exact_quote: The winter sports area is situated between the elevations of 241 and 258 m ... The hill includes seven runs, a chairlift, six rope tows, and a tubing hill ... a quad chair lift and 6 rope tows
notes: Tiny Chicagoland (Bartlett IL) suburban hill operated by Bartlett Park District: ~180ft vert (17m), 7 runs, 1 quad + 6 rope tows = 7 lifts, tubing hill. ORD ~25mi east.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 1000 feet ... 125 acres of skiable terrain ... 8 lifts ... average annual snowfall of 74 inches ... 27 trails ... 100% snowmaking ... 2025-2026 ski season opens November 22nd ... night skiing 7-9:30 PM every night December 25th through March 8th
notes: Central MA day-trip mountain: 1,000ft vert, 125 acres, 27 trails, 8 lifts, 74in avg snow, 100% snowmaking. Strong night skiing (Dec 25-Mar 8). BOS ~55mi east.

exact_quote: The total vertical drop is 780 feet (240 m) ... 200 Acres ... There is one triple chairlift serving the hill, with two landings ... Average Snowfall: 50 Inches ... Twenty-two runs splay out from the summit
notes: Volunteer-run community hill in Lakeview OR's Warner Mountains: 780ft vert, 200 acres, 22 runs, 1 triple chair, 50in snow. LKV Lake County local airfield, RDM Redmond ~150mi NW for commercial.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 2,020 feet ... approximately 265 acres of skiable terrain ... 10 lifts, including two high speed lifts, a quad and a six pack ... average of 124 inches of natural snowfall ... 62 trails ... The resort has five terrain parks ... Gladed areas such as Furgal's Forest, Psyched Out, and Sugar Shack Glades ... opening day to the public is November 29th
notes: Family-oriented NH resort: 2,020ft vert, 265 acres (Green Peak expansion), 62 trails, 10 lifts (incl. six-pack), 124in snow, 5 terrain parks, glades, night skiing Fri/Sat. Base village lodging. MHT ~75mi south.

exact_quote: vertical elevation of approximately 360 feet (110 m) ... 140 acres of skiable terrain ... 11 lifts (soon to be 12), with 7 Quads, 1 Triple, 1 Moving Carpet and 1 Rope Tow ... Average annual snowfall is 45 inches ... 50 trails
notes: MN day-area on Cannon River: 360ft vert, 140 acres, 50 trails, 11 lifts (heavy on quads), 45in snow. MSP ~60mi NW.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 1,010 feet ... 31 trails across 126 acres ... 30 ski trails ranging in difficulty from easy green trails to more advanced black diamond trails and some glades ... night skiing on 15 trails ... replaced all its old chairlifts with newer quad and triple chairlifts
notes: Queensbury NY (Lake George area): 1,010ft vert, 126 acres, 31 trails, glades, night skiing on 15 trails. Quad + triple chairlifts. ALB Albany ~60mi south.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 700 ft (210 m) ... 30 trails, spread over 85 acres (34 ha) of skiable terrain ... four lifts: a double chair, a T-Bar, a magic carpet and a handle tow ... average annual snowfall of 110 inches (280 cm) ... two terrain parks ... glades are all over the mountain ... the only night-skiing option in the Dartmouth/Lake Sunapee region ... typically opens in late December and runs through mid-March
notes: Non-profit Enfield NH community area: 700ft vert, 85 acres, 30 trails, 4 lifts, 110in snow. 2 terrain parks, glades, night skiing. Run by Upper Valley Snow Sports Foundation. LEB Lebanon ~10mi NW, MHT ~80mi SE.

exact_quote: over 50 km of maintained trails ranging from 3,220 below the lodge to 4,463 feet at Tucker County's high point atop Weiss Knob ... White Grass is a cross-country ski touring center without traditional ski lifts ... 150 inches of natural snowfall with 5 kilometres of snow fences
notes: Cross-country ski touring center in Canaan Valley WV (est. 1979). 50+km trails, 3,220-4,463ft elev (1,243ft range), no lifts (XC only), 150in snow. Wholesome natural foods cafe. MGW Morgantown ~110mi N.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 2,000 feet (610 m), with a base elevation at 4,500 feet and a lift-served summit at 6,500 feet ... 1,402 acres of terrain ... five chairlifts: two high speed quads, two fixed-grip quads, and one triple, plus 3 magic carpets ... average of 350 inches of annual snowfall
notes: WA Cascades ski area 53mi west of Yakima on US-12: 2,000ft vert, 1,402 acres, 8 lifts total (5 chairs + 3 carpets), 350in snow. YKM Yakima ~53mi east; SEA Seattle ~140mi NW.

exact_quote: 1,100' Vertical Drop ... operates three lifts—including two triple chairs and one surface lift ... 370 Acres ... averaging 81 inches annually ... base elevation is 8,400' and the summit is 9,500' ... Established in 1940
notes: Small Pinedale WY local hill (est. 1940): 1,100ft vert, 370 acres, 3 lifts (2 triples + 1 surface), 81in snow. JAC Jackson Hole ~80mi NW; RKS Rock Springs ~100mi south.

exact_quote: over 20 kilometers of classic and skate ski track that is groomed daily. The 20K Nordic Track features professionally-groomed loops of 3, 5 and 10 kilometers ... located in the heart of Park City. Its address is 1541 Thaynes Canyon Drive, Park City, UT 84060
notes: Nordic-only XC center in Park City UT (no alpine lifts): 20+km of classic/skate groomed track, loops of 3/5/10km. Located on Park City Golf Course. SLC ~35mi west via I-80.

exact_quote: 43 ski runs across 400 acres ... lies in the Penokee Mountain Range 16 miles off the southern Lake Superior shoreline ... five lifts and 43 runs ... an average of 200 inches of snow per season ... November 2025, the resort filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
notes: Upson WI (Iron County) resort in Penokees near Lake Superior: 400 acres, 43 runs, 5 lifts, 200in lake-effect snow. Self-styled snowiest WI resort. Filed Chapter 11 Nov 2025. IMT Iron Mountain ~85mi NE; DLH Duluth ~110mi NW.

exact_quote: Whiteface's highest lift unloads at 4,386 feet (1,337 m), a vertical drop of 3,166 ft (965 m) to the base area at 1,220 ft (372 m) ... 299 skiable acres not including 35 acres of in-bounds, off-piste double-black diamond wilderness terrain skiing on The Slides ... one gondola, nine chairlifts, and two conveyor lifts ... average snowfall at Whiteface Mountain is 190 inches
notes: Lake Placid NY 1980 Olympic mountain: 3,166ft vert (greatest in East, 3,430ft incl Slides), 299 acres + 35 acres The Slides, 12 lifts (1 gondola + 9 chairs + 2 carpets), 190in snow. SLK Saranac Lake ~25mi west; ALB Albany ~130mi south.

exact_quote: vertical drop of the ski area is 2,353 feet (717 m), with a summit elevation of 6,817 ft (2,078 m) and a base of 4,464 ft (1,361 m) ... 3,020 acres of skiable terrain ... 14 operating lifts ... average annual snowfall is 300 inches (760 cm) ... west of Glacier National Park in the Flathead National Forest
notes: Major MT destination resort on Big Mountain near Glacier NP: 2,353ft vert, 3,020 acres, 14 lifts, 300in snow. Slope-side base village. FCA Glacier Park Intl ~12mi south = one of the closest airports of any major US resort.

exact_quote: 935-foot vertical drop ... 120 acres ... 8 lifts ... 20 trails ... average annual snowfall of around 40 inches ... located on Two Top Mountain in the Tuscarora Mountains of Southern Pennsylvania ... 89 miles from Washington, D.C., and 92 miles from Baltimore
notes: Mercersburg PA day-trip resort (Vail-owned): 935ft vert, 120 acres, 20 trails, 8 lifts, 40in snow + heavy snowmaking. ~90mi from DC/Baltimore. HGR Hagerstown ~25mi south, IAD Dulles ~75mi SE.

exact_quote: Wild Mountain delivers 26 runs and 4 terrain parks across 100 acres of terrain ... Wild Mountain has 8 lifts ... located in Amador Township, Chisago County, near Taylors Falls, Minnesota
notes: Taylors Falls MN (St. Croix valley): 100 acres, 26 runs, 4 terrain parks, 8 lifts, 'Wild Chutes' tubing. MSP ~45mi SW.

exact_quote: vertical drop of 2,112 feet (644 m) is the second largest in New Hampshire and the ninth largest in New England ... 49 trails on 225 acres (91 ha), including the 2.75-mile (4.43 km) Polecat Trail — the longest ski trail in New Hampshire ... four operating chairlifts, with one detachable quad ... 1,950-foot (590 m) base elevation and proximity to Mount Washington produces an annual natural snowfall of over 200 inches
notes: Pinkham Notch NH across from Mt. Washington (Vail/Mountain Capital owned): 2,112ft vert (#2 NH), 225 acres, 49 trails, 4 chairs (1 detachable quad), 2.75mi Polecat (longest NH run), 200in snow. PWM Portland ~85mi SE; MHT ~125mi south.

exact_quote: 1,563 feet of vertical terrain ... 555 Acres ... Six lifts, including a high-speed six-pack ... average snowfall of 430 inches ... 29 downhill runs ... 20 kilometers of Nordic trails ... base elevation of 5,120 feet and a summit elevation of 6,683 feet
notes: OR Cascades resort ~1hr from Eugene: 1,563ft vert, 555 acres, 29 runs, 6 lifts (incl. six-pack), 430in heavy Cascades snow, 20km Nordic. EUG Eugene ~65mi NW.

exact_quote: 450 vertical feet for skiing ... Two chair lifts and four surface lifts ... 50 acres ... 80 inches average annual snowfall ... features both day and night skiing, a tubing park ... established in 1957 ... Known as 'New York's Coolest Little Ski Area'
notes: Greenwich NY tiny family ski area (est. 1957): 450ft vert, 50 acres, 6 lifts (2 chairs + 4 surface), 80in snow, night skiing, tubing park. ALB Albany ~30mi south.

exact_quote: vertical drop of about 200 feet (60 m) ... 135 acres ... 23 runs ... 3 quad chairs, 1 triple chair, 2 double chairs, and 6 surface lifts, totaling 11 chairlifts with a lift capacity of 12,500 people an hour ... about 48 inches of snowfall annually, and snowmaking covers 100 percent of the terrain
notes: Vail-owned Chicagoland day hill in Wilmot WI (just north of IL border, Kenosha County): 200ft vert, 135 acres, 23 runs, 11 lifts (12,500/hr), 48in snow + 100% snowmaking. ORD ~60mi south, MKE ~50mi north.

exact_quote: peaks at 3,100 feet (940 m), with a vertical drop of 1,600 feet (490 m) ... 54 trails on 285 skiable acres 97% of which are covered by snowmaking ... an average of 120 inches of natural snowfall annually ... two separate mountain peaks, known as East Peak and West Peak
notes: Catskills NY resort (recently converted to private Windham Mountain Club): 1,600ft vert, 285 acres, 54 trails, ~12 lifts, 120in snow, 97% snowmaking. Two peaks (East/West). ALB ~50mi north.

exact_quote: base sits at 9,000 ft with 3,060 ft of vertical rise, putting its beautiful summit at 12,060 ft ... 3,081 total skiable acreage (1,246 hectares), includes 166 designated trails and 1,212 acres (490 hectares) of off-piste terrain ... 23 lifts, including three high-speed six-pack lifts, one 10-person gondola ... over 344.6 inches of snow annually ... three interconnected mountain peaks — Winter Park, Mary Jane, and Vasquez Ridge
notes: Major CO destination resort owned by City of Denver/operated by Alterra: 3,060ft vert, 3,081 acres (1,212 of off-piste), 166 trails, 23 lifts (incl. 10-pax gondola), 345in snow. 3 peaks: Winter Park, Mary Jane, Vasquez. DEN ~67mi east on I-70 = closest major Colorado resort to Denver airport.

exact_quote: Vertical Drop: 1,003 feet ... Skiable acres: 129 acres ... 5 lifts with 27 runs ... around 35 inches ... a four-season mountain resort on the eastern slopes of the Blue Ridge Mountains, located in Nelson County, Virginia ... 100 percent snowmaking coverage across its 129 skiable acres ... opened in 1975 and is currently owned and managed by Pacific Group Resorts
notes: Blue Ridge VA four-season resort (Pacific Group Resorts): 1,003ft vert, 129 acres, 27 runs, 5 lifts, 35in natural + 100% snowmaking. Full slopeside lodging/condos. CHO Charlottesville ~39mi NE.

exact_quote: vertical descent is 603 feet (184 m) ... 90 acres of skiable area ... nine lifts ... average snowfall for the resort is 100 inches ... opened in 1983 ... located in Ghent, West Virginia on Raleigh County's Flat Top Mountain ... 28 trails and a terrain park
notes: Ghent WV (Flat Top Mtn) day resort: 603ft vert, 90 acres, 28 trails, 9 lifts, 100in snow, terrain park. BKW Beckley ~10mi north; CRW Charleston ~70mi NW.

exact_quote: Vertical Drop: 700 feet ... 172 acres ... 13 lifts, which include two quad lifts, five triple chairlifts, five conveyor carpets, and one surface tow ... receives on average 100 inches of snow each year ... the only four-season downhill ski resort in Maryland ... located near Deep Creek Lake in the town of McHenry in Garrett County, Maryland
notes: Only ski resort in MD, located at Deep Creek Lake (McHenry): 700ft vert, 172 acres, 13 lifts (2 quads + 5 triples + 5 carpets + 1 tow), 100in snow. Slope-side lodging. PIT Pittsburgh ~100mi (closest major); MGW Morgantown ~30mi closer for regional.

exact_quote: Vertical Drop: 1,604 feet ... 1,600 acres ... Wolf Creek's ten lifts service the terrain ... 430 natural inches, described as 'The Most Snow in Colorado' ... Base elevation of 10,300 feet and summit elevation of 11,904 feet ... longest run is the Navajo Trail at 2 miles
notes: Famous powder destination on Continental Divide (CO): 1,604ft vert, 1,600 acres, 10 lifts, 430in/yr = most snow in CO. 2mi Navajo Trail longest. No on-site lodging. ALS Alamosa ~55mi (closest commercial); DRO Durango ~84mi via Pagosa Springs.

exact_quote: 500-foot vertical drop ... 3 lifts servicing 11 trails ... 180 inches of snowfall per year ... snowmaking at Woods is very good, covering almost 100% of the mountain ... beginner area called 'Easy Acres'
notes: Central NY (Westernville, Oneida County) lake-effect snowbelt: 500ft vert, 11 trails, 3 lifts (2 chairs + 1 surface), 180in lake-effect snow + ~100% snowmaking. SYR Syracuse ~40mi SW.

exact_quote: more than 45 km of skiing right in the town of Woodstock, VT on two trail systems. The Mount Peg trails begin on the golf course at the Woodstock Country Club ... On nearby Mt. Tom, the Center grooms more than 20 km of trails on old carriage roads in the midst of Vermont's first tree farm and Marsh-Billings-Rockefeller National Historical Park ... more than 15 km of trails are left in their natural state for backcountry skiing and snowshoeing
notes: Cross-country center run by Woodstock Inn & Resort, VT (no alpine lifts): 45+km of groomed XC trails over Mt. Peg + Mt. Tom systems, plus 15+km backcountry. Inn lodging available. LEB Lebanon NH ~25mi north; BTV Burlington ~95mi NW.
